title: Counterfactual undercut/overcut value features
summary: Official F1 strategy explainers repeatedly frame the decision as 'pit now' versus 'stay out one more lap' under clean-air, tyre warm-up, and degradation tradeoffs.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explainers repeatedly frame the decision as 'pit now' versus 'stay out one more lap' under clean-air, tyre warm-up, and degradation tradeoffs. That suggests modeling option value directly, not just whether the current tyre can finish.
implementation_hint: Create proxy features for expected pit-now benefit: same-lap peer-relative pace, compound-specific warm-up risk, estimated pit-loss by race, clean-air proxy from position/field context, and one-lap counterfactual margins such as expected gain from fresh tyres minus pit-loss/traffic penalty. Include separate overcut-risk features for hard tyres or cool/wet compounds.
expected_effect: Can capture why two cars with similar tyre age behave differently because one is in undercut range and the other is protected by track position or poor out-lap warm-up.
risk: The dataset lacks explicit gaps, traffic, and safety-car labels, so these counterfactuals are approximations and may be circuit-specific.
